description: OOMKilled pod with clear memory metric in describe output
raw_output:
Name:         web-backend-7d9f8b-xk2p9
Namespace:    production
Node:         node-3/10.0.1.15
Status:       Failed
IP:           172.16.0.45
Controlled By: ReplicaSet/web-backend-7d9f8b
Containers:
  web-backend:
    Container ID: containerd://a3f9...
    Image:        acme/web-backend:v2.3.1
    Limits:
      memory: 512Mi
    Requests:
      memory: 128Mi
    Last State:
      Terminated:
        Reason:    OOMKilled
        Exit Code: 137
        Started:   Mon, 21 Jul 2026 03:47:12 +0000
        Finished:  Mon, 21 Jul 2026 03:52:41 +0000
    Ready:         False
    Restart Count: 7
Conditions:
  Type     Status
  Ready    False
Events:
  Warning  OOMKilling  5m  kubelet  Memory cgroup out of memory: Kill process 4821 (node) score 1982

expected.cause: Container exceeded 512Mi memory limit during operation
expected.root_cause: Pod web-backend-7d9f8b-xk2p9 OOMKilled after 7 restarts — memory limit 512Mi exceeded, likely heap growth

description: EXPLAIN output — no error, no row change, needs inference
raw_output:
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id WHERE orders.status = 'pending';
id | select_type | table     | type | key     | rows   | Extra
1  | SIMPLE      | orders    | ALL  | NULL    | 842000 | Using where
1  | SIMPLE      | customers | ref  | PRIMARY | 1      | NULL

expected.result_summary: Full table scan on orders (842k rows, no index) joined with customers on primary key
expected.root_cause: EXPLAIN reveals full table scan on orders table — 842k rows scanned with no index on status column
expected.metric_impact: 842000 rows scanned, no index used on orders.status

description: Kubernetes deployment YAML with misconfigured resource limits
raw_output:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-backend
  namespace: production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web-backend
        image: acme/web-backend:v2.3.1
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
        env:
        - name: DB_HOST
          value: postgres.production.svc.cluster.local
        - name: CACHE_HOST
          value: redis.production.svc.cluster.local

anomalies: memory request (128Mi) is 4x less than limit (512Mi) — large burst ratio may cause OOM on traffic spikes
expected.root_cause: Deployment web-backend memory request 128Mi vs limit 512Mi — 4x ratio high, likely cause OOM events
